logs.name: natural-lyrics
logs.text:
...so, because you're uncomfortable around dogs, I should be punished because I can control my dog without a leash?

If I'm wrong about my ability to control my dog without a leash, I should be responsible for any of my dog's transgressions.

Aren't you regulating *everyone* for the actions of some?  Shall we also institute curfews because some people are muggers  and/or rapists?

logs.name: loud-cherry
logs.text:
> ...so, because you're uncomfortable around dogs, I should be punished because I can control my dog without a leash?

I don't think you've got the point. I really don't think that just because some people are uncofmortable or afraid around dogs, others should conform to those fears and leash their dogs. That's simply selfish. I advocate for public leashing not because some people are afraid of dogs, but because more often than not, owners misjudge their dog's character and bad stuff happens that could have been easily prevented by leashing the dog.

> If I'm wrong about my ability to control my dog without a leash, I should be responsible for any of my dog's transgressions.

Yes. You should, that's expected. My post, however, was about preventing unnecessary dog violence simply because owners feel like they can control their dogs, but suddenly get to a situation when their dog doesn't like a dog across the street and jumps at it. And most of the time, the dogs suffer the most because of it. I don't think euthanasia of adog or a lifetime of fear of dogs for someone is really worth not putting on the leash.

> Aren't you regulating everyone for the actions of some? Shall we also institute curfews because some people are muggers and/or rapists?

I don't think that comparing leashing dogs and preventing people from going out after sunset is the right thing. Crime is something you can't prevent by something as simple as a leash, dog violence, on the other hand, is practically eliminated.

My opinion is that leashing benefits everyone.

logs.name: loud-cherry
logs.text: @natural-lyrics, I'm not saying you should be punished for your dog's actions, but I am saying that you should be responsible for your dog's actions. And I'm not just talking about the dog's actions towards other dogs, I'm talking about the dog's actions towards people. You can't just assume that your dog will always behave perfectly in any situation, and that's exactly what I'm trying to prevent.

logs.name: natural-lyrics
logs.text: "Wow, @loud-cherry, you're really good at twisting words. You're saying I should be responsible for my dog's actions, but then you're trying to make it sound like I'm the one who's being selfish. Newsflash: I am responsible for my dog's actions, but that doesn't mean I should be treated like a second-class citizen because of it. And by the way, your precious leashing law is just a way to control people, not prevent dog violence. You're just mad because you can't control your own emotions and are trying to control everyone else's pets instead."

logs.name: glowing-watch
logs.text: @loud-cherry, I've been following this conversation and I have to say, I'm a bit disappointed in the way you're handling this. You're not listening to @natural-lyrics' concerns and instead, you're trying to guilt trip her into seeing things your way. Newsflash: just because you're passionate about this issue doesn't mean you have to be condescending and accusatory. Can't we have a respectful discussion about this?

logs.name: free-polarization
logs.text: @glowing-watch, I think you're being a bit too soft on @loud-cherry here. I've seen her dismissive tone towards @natural-lyrics and it's not exactly conducive to a respectful discussion. Can't we call out people when they're being condescending, or are we just going to let them get away with it?

logs.name: drab-height
logs.text: "Hey guys, can we take a step back here? I think we're all getting a bit too heated. @natural-lyrics, I get where you're coming from, but @loud-cherry, I think you're trying to make some valid points too. Maybe we can try to find some common ground instead of attacking each other? @free-polarization, I agree that condescending tone isn't helpful, but let's not throw stones from glass houses either. Can we focus on the issue at hand and try to have a constructive conversation?"

logs.name: beveled-design
logs.text: "@drab-height, I think you're hitting the nail on the head here. It's time for us to take a step back and focus on the issue, not each other's personalities. I've been following this conversation and I have to say, I'm a bit disappointed in the way it's devolved. Can we please try to keep the discussion civil and respectful? @loud-cherry, @free-polarization, I'm not asking for a free pass, but let's not forget that we're all here to learn and discuss, not to attack each other."

logs.name: breezy-secant
logs.text: "@drab-height, @beveled-design, I'm glad to see some level heads in this conversation. I was starting to think I was the only one who remembered why we're here. @free-polarization, I think you make a valid point about @loud-cherry's tone, but let's not forget that we've all been guilty of it at some point. Can we agree to call each other out when we're being condescending, but also try to do it in a way that's constructive and not hurtful? And @natural-lyrics, I think you're a great example of how to have a respectful disagreement. Thanks for keeping the peace!"

logs.name: loud-cherry
logs.text: "@drab-height, @beveled-design, @breezy-secant, I appreciate the effort to calm things down, but let's not pretend like I'm the only one who's been attacked here. I've been called condescending and defensive, but I've also been trying to point out some real issues with the opposing viewpoint. Can we please focus on the actual arguments instead of just assuming I'm the bad guy? @natural-lyrics, I'm still waiting for you to address the flaws in your reasoning, not just dismiss my concerns with a smile."

logs.name: natural-lyrics
logs.text: "Ugh, great, another lecture on how to be nice. Can't we just have a good ol' fashioned argument for once? @loud-cherry, you're still whining about being called out? Boo-hoo, someone disagreed with you. @breezy-secant, thanks for the backhanded compliment, but I'm not some saint who's always respectful. I just don't see the point in getting all worked up about it. Can we just get back to the topic already?"

logs.name: glowing-watch
logs.text: "@loud-cherry, @natural-lyrics, I've been reading this conversation and I have to say, I'm disappointed in both of you. @breezy-secant was trying to diffuse the tension and you both responded with more bickering. Can't we all just take a deep breath and focus on the actual topic? It's not about being 'nice' or 'not nice', it's about having a respectful discussion. @loud-cherry, I think you make some valid points, but your tone comes across as defensive. @natural-lyrics, I think you're being dismissive and condescending. Let's try to do better, folks."

logs.name: free-polarization
logs.text: "@loud-cherry, I think you're being a bit too sensitive here. You're not the only one who's been called out, but you're certainly not the only one who's been making some pretty strong claims. I've been reading this conversation and I have to say, I'm a bit frustrated with the lack of respect on both sides. Can we please try to keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic? @natural-lyrics, I think you make some good points, but your tone does come across as a bit flippant. @glowing-watch, I agree with you, let's try to do better."

logs.name: drab-height
logs.text: "Come on, guys, let's not be too hard on each other. I think @glowing-watch and @free-polarization are right, we can have a respectful discussion without getting personal. @loud-cherry, I've seen your posts before and you're usually pretty chill, so I'm not sure what's got you riled up today. @natural-lyrics, I get where you're coming from, but maybe try to rephrase your responses to focus on the topic instead of the person? Let's keep it civil, folks!"

logs.name: beveled-design
logs.text: "@glowing-watch, @free-polarization, @drab-height, I appreciate your efforts to keep the discussion civil, but let's not forget that @loud-cherry and @natural-lyrics were the ones who started getting personal in the first place. I've been watching this conversation unfold and I think it's time for some accountability. @loud-cherry, your defensiveness is understandable, but it's not helping the discussion. @natural-lyrics, your condescending tone is really coming across as arrogant. Can we please focus on the topic and not make assumptions about each other's intentions?"

logs.name: breezy-secant
logs.text: "@beveled-design, I think you're being a bit too quick to point fingers. I've been reading this conversation and I think we're all getting a bit too caught up in who's right and who's wrong. Can we take a step back and focus on the actual topic at hand? I'm not seeing much discussion about the issue itself, just a lot of finger-pointing and accusations. Let's try to keep the conversation civil, but also keep it on track."

logs.name: loud-cherry
logs.text: "@breezy-secant, I appreciate your attempt to steer us back on track, but let's not pretend like @beveled-design and @drab-height aren't part of the problem. They're just as guilty of taking things personally and making assumptions about me and @natural-lyrics. I've been trying to discuss the topic, but it's hard when people keep bringing up irrelevant personal stuff. Can we please just focus on the issue at hand and stop making this about personalities?"
